title: PocketForge canonical app descriptor (app.toml)
description: THE reconciled app.toml — ONE descriptor, THREE consumers (the on-device broker/supervisor at launch; the packaging path at build/sign; the SDK/author tooling at scaffold). Reconciles the three pre-E8 schemas: Phase-1 plan pocketforge-plan.md §9.3/§9.4 ([app]/[launch]/[health]/[fetch]); Phase-2 OCI build-integration-reference.md §18 (the sibling OCI/signature/SBOM/slice files); and pf-broker manifest.rs ([app].use=[] authority graph). E8 (tsp-ziac.1) ADDS the [runtime] platform-family pin (family + abi + platform-version), which none of the three carried. The OCI/signature/SBOM/resource-limit layer is a set of SIBLING FILES beside app.toml (see docs/PLATFORM-ABI-CONTRACT.md), not keys in this file.

app.description: Identity + the capability authority graph. `use=[]` is the CEILING of what the app may ever acquire (pf-broker manifest.rs); the broker refuses anything outside it at runtime.

id.description: reverse-DNS-ish app id; matched by the broker + the blessed-binary registry

use.description: Capability authority graph: `<cap>[:<modifier>][?]` tokens. `?` = OPTIONAL (graceful HardwareAbsent if the device can't back it). The AUTHORITATIVE capability-universe + descriptor-backed dangling check is runtime/crates/pf-broker/src/manifest.rs (cooperative, on-device). The platform static validator checks only token well-formedness (see core/appmanifest.py).

runtime.description: The per-SoC-family Platform ABI pin (E8 / tsp-ziac.1). A Platform accepts an app iff app.runtime.family == platform.family (or an accepted alias) AND app.runtime.abi is offered. `platform-version` pins the exact frozen {kernel,gpu,sdl} SHA-set via abi/platform-abi.json.

family.description: canonical family id or accepted alias — see abi/families.toml (e.g. pocketforge/a133-powervr, pocketforge/a523-mali)

abi.description: the frozen libpocketforge/PFW1 contract version (STABILITY.md); currently "1"

platform-version.description: the frozen Platform release this build pins → resolves to a {kernel,gpu,sdl} SHA-set in abi/platform-abi.json for this family. Optional but recommended for reproducibility/provenance.

fetch.description: Optional per-app first-boot HTTPS binary fetch (plan §9.4). NOTE: distinct from the vendor-BLOB manifest.toml [[blobs]] IPFS fetch (image/scripts/ipfs-fetch.sh) — same word, different mechanism; they are NOT unified.
